################################################################################
# batch_an.txt — gameplay C1->C2 drain (campaign batch 3 of ~5)
#
# Generated by the discover-c1-batch skill, 2026-06-03.
# Work type:    promote-c2 (C1 -> C2 mechanical promotion). AUTHOR-ONLY fanout.
# Sessions:     6  (Mashed_pool1,2,3,7,8,10 pre-assigned)
#               -> pool0,4,5,6 SKIPPED: all four carry an ACTIVE .lock right now
#                  (pool4 chronic; pool0/5/6 leaked in-JVM locks during prior
#                  fanouts — see memory feedback_mcp_leaked_project_lock). The six
#                  assigned slots are the ones that completed batch_al + batch_am
#                  cleanly.
# Total RVAs:   156  (gameplay, next-156 by address 0x00456040..0x0047f380)
# Model:        Opus 4.8 (1M context)  (claude-opus-4-8[1m])
#               -> promote-c2 bulk-drain cap 60 RVAs/session (this batch: 26 each).
#               Same author-only cadence as batch_aj/ak/al/am (Opus author-only
#               fanout + scripted ghidra-sweep + central re-classify).
#
# CAMPAIGN CONTEXT: gameplay is now a 480-RVA C1 pool (was 792; batch_al drained the
# lowest 156 -> 636, batch_am the next 156 -> 480). This is batch 3 of ~5. It takes
# the NEXT 156 by address above 0x00455fe0 (the 0x00456-0x0045d dense cluster +
# a 0x00461-0x0047f tail). batch_am observed the 0x0041e-0x00422 + 0x00449-0x00455
# region split ~60/156 to render/audio/hud (effect-entity, debris/smoke particle,
# in-world-item audio accessors, lock-on reticle). EXPECT a similar mix here:
# the 0x0045-0x0047 pages mix race/entity game-logic with RenderWare effect/
# scene-graph plumbing (clump/atomic/material/frame) and collision/camera helpers.
# Many "gameplay" candidates will reclassify to render / audio / hud / effects on
# decomp. That's fine — they leave gameplay C1 regardless. Confirm per RVA and
# record subsystem_observed. render (458) remains the OTHER campaign, deferred (its
# 0x004e/0x0053/0x0054/0x0055 pages are RenderWare-core-heavy and need their own
# library-aware batches).
#
# Pre-flight (verified at generation time, 2026-06-03):
#   * re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Queued = EMPTY. batch_am fully swept + re-classified
#     (sweep-20260603-0427; commits d682c50b drain + 6822bb6c release + 7145c072
#     re-classify). gameplay C1 636 -> 480. SAFE to fan out. (If a sweep is running
#     when you start, STOP and re-confirm.)
#   * Library-band screen over all 156 candidates: ZERO hits (CRT 0x004a0000-
#     0x004b3fff | D3DX9-PSGP 0x004ec000-0x004fc9e0 | qhull 0x0057c5b0-0x005a5820).
#     Still apply the library-confirm rule if any RVA decodes as a named
#     RenderWare/CRT primitive (note it, do NOT rename, set library_skip=<rva>:<tag>).
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AUTHOR-ONLY PROTOCOL (read before any session runs) — identical to batch_aj/ak/al/am
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   * DO write per-RVA .md plates into the session's bucket dir, using the
#     template heading **`## Mechanical description`** (NOT `## Purpose` — batch_aj
#     s3 used `## Purpose` and the sweep manifest builder returned 0 plated for
#     that bucket until renamed. This heading is a HARD requirement.)
#   * DO append ONE row to a PER-SESSION queue fragment:
#         re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s<N>.md   (NOT the shared re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md)
#     The queued row MUST use the field name **`rvas=`** for everything the sweep
#     should plate (NOT `rvas_c2=` — batch_ak s3 used `rvas_c2=` and the builder
#     could not see it). If you have library-skips, put genuine-game RVAs in
#     `rvas=` and list vendored ones in a SEPARATE `library_skip=<rva>:<tag>,...`
#     field for the central re-classify (they are NOT plated by the sweep).
#   * DO NOT write hooks.csv. DO NOT run re-classify. DO NOT touch STUBS.md /
#     UNCERTAINTIES.md / DEFERRED.md / re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md directly.
#   * DO NOT mutate the master Ghidra project. Read-only MCP only (function_at,
#     decomp_function, listing_*). function_create / renames are the SWEEP's job —
#     if an RVA has no function object, write the plate from the raw listing and
#     set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row.
#   * DO NOT mint U-IDs or S-IDs. Mark new holes inline as bare `[UNCERTAIN]` in
#     the plate's `## Uncertainties` section with the gap described; central
#     re-classify mints from the reserved range (this batch: U-8300..U-8599,
#     S-6600..S-6799).
#   * Each session reads ONLY its pre-assigned pool clone (read-only). One commit
#     at end of session (plates + queue fragment), message:
#         "scribe(batch_an s<N>): author <K> C1->C2 plates for gameplay <range>"
#     Commit ATOMICALLY and path-scoped: `git commit -- <your bucket dir> <your
#     fragment>` — do NOT `git add -A`/`git add .` (a sibling parallel session may
#     have staged unrelated files; see memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #5,
#     the batch_aj-s5 shared-working-copy index race).
#
# DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #7):
#   Before doing ANY work, check whether your bucket dir / fragment / pool marker
#   already exists (another terminal launched the same session). If so, STOP and
#   yield — do NOT overwrite. If you already wrote an early file that the owner
#   keeps, yield by `git restore`, NEVER blind-`rm`.
#
# FOLLOW-UP (single Opus session on main, AFTER all 6 commit — do NOT run now):
#   Scripted ghidra-sweep:
#     1. py -3.12 scripts/sweep_build_manifest.py --rows re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s1.md
#        ... re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s6.md --out sweep_manifest.json --date <D> --level C2
#        (confirm per-bucket plated == queued rva counts AND missing_md==0).
#     2. ghidra_eval apply via scripts/ghidra/sweep_apply_eval.py (one txn).
#     3. Central re-classify on main: C1->C2 for plated RVAs; honor subsystem_observed
#        reclassifications; reclass-OUT any library_skip RVAs to
#        third-party-library[...] (kept-C1); mint U-/S- IDs from the reserved ranges.
#        Merge fragments into SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Drained.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POOL-SLOT HYGIENE (every session)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_an_s<N>"      # per-session marker, no race
#   Use the PRE-ASSIGNED Mashed_pool<S> clone directly (do NOT call
#   ghidra_pool.ps1 acquire). pool0/4/5/6 are SKIPPED (active .lock at generation
#   time; see memory feedback_mcp_leaked_project_lock). If your assigned slot is
#   stale/leaked-locked, fall back to ANOTHER verified-free clone (NOT pool0/4/5/6;
#   try pool9/11/12/13...), and record the ACTUAL slot used in the queue row.
#   ALWAYS program_close at end.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUBSYSTEM HINT (not a claim)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   Working hypothesis for all 156: gameplay. The 0x00456-0x0045d + 0x00461-0x0047f
#   region mixes game-state/race/entity logic with on-screen-effect / scene-graph
#   rendering (RenderWare clump/atomic/material/frame) and collision/camera helpers.
#   Based on batch_am, EXPECT a meaningful share to reclass to render / audio / hud /
#   effects. CONFIRM per RVA from decomp and record subsystem_observed=<X> in the
#   queue row when it differs. The sweep + central re-classify reconcile.
################################################################################


================================================================================
Session 1 — bucket_gameplay_00456040_004588c0  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool1 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00456040_004588c0/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s1.md  (per-session fragment; sweep merges later)

# Mission
First slice of the gameplay batch-3 pool (0x00456040-0x004588c0). Read each
end-to-end and write a mechanical C2 plate (full control flow, every offset/constant
cited, callees + callers noted). Working hypothesis: gameplay; expect some
render/audio/hud/effects reclassification. Confirm or reclassify per RVA.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA: read the path in that RVA's hooks.csv `file` column
  FIRST (deepen, don't restart). Drift-skip any already >= C2.
- Cross-reference: Session 2 (next gameplay range).

# Workflow
1. DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (above). Then export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_an_s1";
   use Mashed_pool1 read-only.
2. For each RVA: function_at -> decomp_function (-> listing_code_unit_at if no fn;
   set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row if no function object).
3. Write re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00456040_004588c0/<rva>.md with frontmatter
   (rva, name, size_bytes, confidence_target=C2, callees_depth1, callers_noted,
   opened_in_slot, session_date=2026-06-03) + "## Mechanical description" +
   "## Constants" table + "## Uncertainties" (bare [UNCERTAIN], do NOT mint) +
   "## Stubs encountered" (note, do NOT mint).
4. Append ONE row to re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s1.md: date, an_s1, bucket, rvas=<csv of
   plated>, confidence=C1->C2, note=<subsystem_observed + reclassifications +
   library_skip=... + needs_function_create=... + actual pool slot>.
5. One atomic path-scoped commit: "scribe(batch_an s1): author 26 C1->C2 plates
   for gameplay 00456040_004588c0".

# Candidates (26)
00456040,00456140,004568a0,004568d0,00456c30,00456c70,00456c90,00456ce0,00456d00,00456eb0,00456f70,004570a0,00457100,00457610,00457650,004576b0,004577f0,00457ba0,00457c10,00458020,00458080,00458360,004584e0,00458570,00458880,004588c0

# STOP-AND-ASK if
- function_at AND listing_code_unit_at both null — surface + skip.
- An RVA is a library family (memcpy/_ftol2/RW primitive) — note in plate, do NOT
  rename; set subsystem_observed=third-party-library[...] + library_skip=<rva>:<tag>
  in the queue row.


================================================================================
Session 2 — bucket_gameplay_00458a40_0045ac40  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool2 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00458a40_0045ac40/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s2.md

# Mission
Second gameplay slice (0x00458a40-0x0045ac40). Working hypothesis: gameplay;
expect render/audio/hud/effects reclassification. Confirm/reclassify per RVA.

# Workflow
Identical to Session 1, but POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_an_s2", Mashed_pool2,
bucket dir bucket_gameplay_00458a40_0045ac40/, queue fragment
re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s2.md, commit "scribe(batch_an s2): author 26 C1->C2 plates
for gameplay 00458a40_0045ac40".

# Candidates (26)
00458a40,00458b10,00458c70,00458d00,00458dd0,00458e00,00458f20,00458f80,00458fa0,00458fd0,00459000,004593b0,00459400,00459480,00459540,004595c0,00459620,0045a0d0,0045a110,0045a130,0045a190,0045a3a0,0045a530,0045a590,0045a950,0045ac40

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


================================================================================
Session 3 — bucket_gameplay_0045ae80_0045dd50  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool3 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_0045ae80_0045dd50/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s3.md

# Mission
Third gameplay slice (0x0045ae80-0x0045dd50). Working hypothesis: gameplay;
expect render/audio/hud/effects reclassification. Confirm/reclassify per RVA.
(NB: collision/raycast helpers FUN_0045bfe0/FUN_0045c350 + Bezier::Interpolate
0045c350 sit NEAR this range but are already mapped — they are NOT in the
candidate set; do not collide.)

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_an_s3", Mashed_pool3, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_0045ae80_0045dd50/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s3.md,
commit "scribe(batch_an s3): author 26 C1->C2 plates for gameplay 0045ae80_0045dd50".

# Candidates (26)
0045ae80,0045b360,0045b390,0045bfd0,0045bff0,0045c030,0045c110,0045c330,0045c4e0,0045c510,0045c550,0045c640,0045c850,0045c860,0045c880,0045ca30,0045cab0,0045caf0,0045cb20,0045cbe0,0045d080,0045d0e0,0045da90,0045daf0,0045dce0,0045dd50

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


================================================================================
Session 4 — bucket_gameplay_0045dff0_0046dd90  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool7 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_0045dff0_0046dd90/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s4.md

# Mission
Fourth gameplay slice. NOTE the wide address span: 0x0045dff0 then a sparse walk
across 0x00461-0x0046d (no contiguous gameplay-C1 rows between — the intervening
addresses are already promoted or other subsystems). These 26 are the next
gameplay-C1 by address regardless. Working hypothesis: gameplay; expect
render/audio/hud/effects reclassification. Confirm/reclassify per RVA.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_an_s4", Mashed_pool7, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_0045dff0_0046dd90/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s4.md,
commit "scribe(batch_an s4): author 26 C1->C2 plates for gameplay 0045dff0_0046dd90".

# Candidates (26)
0045dff0,00461e90,00462760,004656e0,00465f40,00466000,00466100,004670a0,004671d0,004672d0,0046bce0,0046bd20,0046bd60,0046bda0,0046be10,0046be50,0046bf50,0046bfc0,0046d2e0,0046d320,0046d360,0046d660,0046d740,0046d880,0046dd80,0046dd90

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.

Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s5.md

# Mission
Fifth gameplay slice. NOTE the wide span: 0x00471430-0x004733b0 dense, then a jump
to 0x004744a0 and the 0x0047b480-0x0047b6b0 tail (intervening addresses already
promoted/other-subsystem). Working hypothesis: gameplay; expect
render/audio/hud/effects reclassification. Confirm/reclassify per RVA.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_an_s5", Mashed_pool8, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_00471430_0047b6b0/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s5.md,
commit "scribe(batch_an s5): author 26 C1->C2 plates for gameplay 00471430_0047b6b0".

# Candidates (26)

Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s6.md

# Mission
Sixth gameplay slice (0x0047ba20-0x0047f380). Working hypothesis: gameplay;
expect render/audio/hud/effects reclassification. Confirm/reclassify per RVA. Closes
the next-156 slice of the gameplay campaign (batch 3/~5).

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_an_s6", Mashed_pool10, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_0047ba20_0047f380/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_an_s6.md,
commit "scribe(batch_an s6): author 26 C1->C2 plates for gameplay 0047ba20_0047f380".

# Candidates (26)
0047ba20,0047bb10,0047bc90,0047c1f0,0047c230,0047c270,0047c2d0,0047ccb0,0047cd50,0047cdc0,0047cde0,0047ce00,0047ce20,0047ce70,0047ce80,0047cf20,0047d130,0047d150,0047d180,0047d330,0047d640,0047def0,0047f1e0,0047f230,0047f290,0047f380

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


################################################################################
# END batch_an.txt — 156 RVAs across 6 author-only sessions (gameplay campaign 3/~5).
# After all 6 commit: run the scripted ghidra-sweep on main (see header), then
# central re-classify C1->C2 + merge per-session queue fragments into
# re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Drained. Expected: gameplay C1 480 -> ~324 (minus any
# reclassified to render/audio/hud/effects/boot/save). Remaining campaigns after
# this slice: gameplay (~324) + render (458).
################################################################################
